name: Mary's Kaeskuch (Cheese Kuchen)
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Half-and-half, light cream or evaporated milk
recipeInstructions:
1. For the Crust: In a large bowl, stir together 1 cup flour, baking powder, 1 1/2 teaspoons sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Cut in butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Make a well in the center and add milk all at once. Stir just until the dough clings together. Place on a lightly floured surface and roll into an 11-inch circle. Fit into the bottom and up sides of an ungreased 9 x 1-1/2 inches round baking pan. Trim the crust even with the top edge of the pan. Bake at 350°F for 5 minutes. Set aside.
2. For the Filling: In a large bowl, beat eggs. Stir in cottage cheese, 1/3 cup sugar, half-and-half, 1 tablespoon flour, vanilla, cinnamon, and 1/8 teaspoon salt until combined. Pour filling into the crust.
3. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes or until the center is nearly set when gently lifted with a spatula. Cool the dessert on a wire rack for 1 hour. Cover and chill for at least 4 hours.
NOTES: Mary Thrill of Fredonia, Wisconsin says this is a longtime favorite Luxembourgish dessert. Serve with coffee for a wonderful midmorning break.
